name: Xvibe
description: Xvibe is an Offline MP3 Player embedded with a dense neural network classifier that classifies songs into vibes and is later used by Xvibe to organize, randomly recommend a list of songs, and track how your songs are distributed into categories, which are acoustic, chaotic, road trip, energetic, and chill.

name: Music Vibe Classification Model for Xvibe
description: This model is integrated into my project, called Xvibe. It classifies songs into the following categories: Acoustic (Acoustic), Chaotic (Rock, Metal), Road Trip (Hip-Hop, Reggae, R&B), Energetic (Country, Disco, Pop), and Chill (Blues, Jazz). The model achieved approximately 80% classification accuracy. However, its accuracy is slightly lower in the application because inference is performed entirely offline, requiring optimizations to accommodate device hardware constraints.

name: Genre Classification Model using Dense Neural Network
description: My first ever model that I trained and built using TensorFlow, supervised learning, transfer learning using the YAMNet model from Google for embeddings, and the GTZAN Dataset. The Model classifies 10 genres among these genres: blues, classical, country, disco, hip-hop, jazz, metal, pop, reggae, and rock, with an accuracy of 75% on each classification.

name: Portfolio
description: A bento-style portfolio inspired by @awsmd and @hyoaru, made to showcase my projects, the technologies I work with, and my skills. Built using React, JavaScript, Tailwind CSS and Vite.

name: Fumimori
description: Fumimori is a Google Keep–inspired note-taking web application that emphasizes minimalist design and essential note-taking functionalities to provide a more intuitive user experience.

name: Overload Gig Store
description: A simple virtual storefront web application that I built to practice front-end, showcasing the merchandise of Overload's Gig annual event called October Praise that takes place in First United Methodist Church, at Bancal, Guagua, Pampanga. The website is built using Vanilla CSS for design, Vanilla JS for interactivity, and deployed via Netlify.

name: Barangay Management and Information System
description: A desktop application I developed to further hone my programming skills while addressing real-world community needs. The project simplifies the management of barangay resident records and automates the processing of requested documents. Key features include tracking of male and female residents, active voters, and households; resident information management; documentation of barangay issues such as blotters; and automated document generation using predefined templates.

name: Enrollment Management System
description: My first-ever project, developed during my 1st year in college, focused on automating the student enrollment process. It streamlined key tasks such as enrolling students, assigning teachers, and tracking student balances. Through this project, I gained valuable experience in writing database queries, applying Object-Oriented Programming, and implementing business rules within a system.
